004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.

006 * "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with the
007 * License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *

010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.http.header;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Utils.*;
020
021import java.util.*;
022import java.util.function.*;
023
024import org.apache.juneau.commons.http.*;
025
026
027/**
028 * Base type for headers whose wire format is an <c>Accept</c>-style list of media ranges.
029 *
030 * <p>
031 * <b>Beta — API subject to change:</b> This type is part of the next-generation REST client and HTTP stack
032 * ({@code org.apache.juneau.marshall.ng.*}).
033 * It is not API-frozen: binary- and source-incompatible changes may appear in the <b>next major</b> Juneau release
034 * (and possibly earlier).
035 *
036 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
037 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/NextGenRestClient">juneau-ng REST client</a>
038 * </ul>
039 *
040 * @since 9.2.1
041 */
042@SuppressWarnings({
043   "java:S2160", // equals() on HttpHeaderBean uses name + getValue(); typed state is reflected in getValue()
044   "unchecked" // Supplier<?> branches cast to typed suppliers after lazy-mode check
045})
046public class HttpMediaRangesHeader extends HttpHeaderBean {
047
048   /** Lazy supplier provides the wire string. */
049   public static final int LAZY_WIRE_STRING = 0;
050
051   /** Lazy supplier provides {@link MediaRanges} (re-evaluated on each access). */
052   public static final int LAZY_MEDIA_RANGES = 1;
053
054   private final MediaRanges cachedForStringOrDirect;
055   private final Supplier<?> lazySupplier;
056   private final int lazyMode;
057
058   /**
059    * @param name Header name. Must not be {@code null}.
060    * @param value Wire value. May be {@code null}.
061    */
062   protected HttpMediaRangesHeader(String name, String value) {
063      super(name, value);
064      this.cachedForStringOrDirect = value == null ? null : MediaRanges.of(value);
065      this.lazySupplier = null;
066      this.lazyMode = -1;
067   }
068
069   /**
070    * @param name Header name. Must not be {@code null}.
071    * @param value Parsed media ranges. May be {@code null}.
072    */
073   protected HttpMediaRangesHeader(String name, MediaRanges value) {
074      super(name, value == null ? null : value.toString());
075      this.cachedForStringOrDirect = value;
076      this.lazySupplier = null;
077      this.lazyMode = -1;
078   }
079
080   /**
081    * @param name Header name. Must not be {@code null}.
082    * @param supplier Lazy supplier; mode {@link #LAZY_WIRE_STRING} or {@link #LAZY_MEDIA_RANGES}. Must not be {@code null}.
083    * @param lazyMode Discriminator for erasure-safe lazy construction.
084    */
085   protected HttpMediaRangesHeader(String name, Supplier<?> supplier, int lazyMode) {
086      super(name, lazyMode == LAZY_WIRE_STRING
087         ? ((Supplier<String>) supplier)::get
088         : () -> {
089            var m = ((Supplier<MediaRanges>) supplier).get();
090            if (m == null)
091               return null;
092            return m.toString();
093         });
094      this.cachedForStringOrDirect = null;
095      this.lazySupplier = supplier;
096      this.lazyMode = lazyMode;
097   }
098
099   /**
100    * @return Parsed {@link MediaRanges}, or {@code null} if the wire value is absent.
101    */
102   public Optional<MediaRanges> asMediaRanges() {
103      return opt(toMediaRanges());
104   }
105
106   /**
107    * @param index Range index.
108    * @return The {@link MediaRange} at {@code index}, or {@code null} if out of range.
109    */
110   public MediaRange getRange(int index) {
111      var x = toMediaRanges();
112      return x == null ? null : x.getRange(index);
113   }
114
115   /**
116    * @param part Subtype fragment to find (e.g. {@code "activity"} for {@code text/json+activity}).
117    * @return {@code true} if any range's subtype contains the fragment.
118    */
119   public boolean hasSubtypePart(String part) {
120      var x = toMediaRanges();
121      return x != null && x.hasSubtypePart(part);
122   }
123
124   /**
125    * @param mediaTypes Candidate types, in preference order.
126    * @return Index of the best matching type, or {@code -1}.
127    */
128   public int match(List<? extends MediaType> mediaTypes) {
129      var x = toMediaRanges();
130      return x == null ? -1 : x.match(mediaTypes);
131   }
132
133   /**
134    * @param other Fallback when no parsed value is present.
135    * @return Parsed ranges or {@code other}.
136    */
137   public MediaRanges orElse(MediaRanges other) {
138      var x = toMediaRanges();
139      return nn(x) ? x : other;
140   }
141
142   /**
143    * @return Parsed {@link MediaRanges}, or {@code null} if the wire value is absent.
144    */
145   public MediaRanges toMediaRanges() {
146      if (lazyMode == LAZY_MEDIA_RANGES)
147         return ((Supplier<MediaRanges>) lazySupplier).get();
148      if (cachedForStringOrDirect != null)
149         return cachedForStringOrDirect;
150      var v = super.getValue();
151      return v == null ? null : MediaRanges.of(v);
152   }
153}
